title: Tags, Versions, Releases, and Provenance · Repository Repair Bay
summary: Prepare a release candidate whose source identity, version, evidence, and published reference can be independently verified. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a signed-off release evidence record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defe…
objectives: Distinguish lightweight and annotated tag objects, messages, taggers, targets, and intended use.
Create, inspect, verify, delete, and cautiously replace tags while recognizing immutable-release expectations.
Apply an explicit version scheme and connect version changes to compatibility, release notes, and source identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Release v4.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits. Competency probe: Distinguish lightweight and annotated tag objects, messages, taggers, targets, and intended use.

Inspect the Lightweight Annotated Tags trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish lightweight and annotated tag objects, messages, taggers, targets, and intended use.

content.text: For Lightweight Annotated Tags, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Lightweight Annotated Tags precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits. Competency probe: Distinguish lightweight and annotated tag objects, messages, taggers, targets, and intended use.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Every tag stores a message and tagger identity.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Lightweight Annotated Tags, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Lightweight Annotated Tags record says a signed-off release evidence record looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect the failure record.

For inspect the failure record, apply Lightweight Annotated Tags without its stated preconditions, omit the a signed-off release evidence record trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Lightweight Annotated Tags for a signed-off release evidence record.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-signature-trust-10h61p" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Signature Trust into observable performance evidence.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Signature Trust: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Release v2.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits. Competency probe: Distinguish commit or tag signature validity, signer identity, key trust, account verification, and policy compliance.
steps.why: Retrieving Signature Trust through the concrete a signed-off release evidence record case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish commit or tag signature validity, signer identity, key trust, account verification, and policy compliance.
